GAMES.d: The definitive competitive shooter — still the benchmark for tactical gunplay after 20 years.
Best FPS campaign ever made. Wall-running, time manipulation, emotional storytelling — all in one package.
Co-op zombie shooter that still holds up. Couch splitscreen, four survivors, Director AI that changes every run.
Clone Wars feeling still unmatched. Couch splitscreen, galactic conquest, hero units — a childhood icon.
Controversial at launch, great today. Splitscreen arcade mode brings solid couch fun back.
Cinematic bullet-time masterpiece. Sao Paulo setting, phenomenal gunplay, unforgettable HEALTH soundtrack.
Revolutionized the medium. Physics puzzles, seamless storytelling — still a benchmark for environmental design.
Rapture is one of gaming's greatest achievements. Philosophical FPS asking what happens when ideology runs unchecked.
Columbia in the sky. Mind-bending story, exceptional atmosphere — the ending will sit with you for days.
The definitive Halo. 4-player couch co-op campaign, legendary multiplayer, split-screen at its peak.
Defined the console shooter. Two-player couch campaign that became a rite of passage for a generation.
Open world Halo with free-to-play multiplayer. Grapple hook changes traversal completely. Co-op campaign included.
Horror shooter with AI that still impresses today. Bullet-time combat that influenced countless games after it.
The arena deathmatch king. Pure skill, pure speed, zero compromise — foundation of competitive FPS.
Roguelite third-person co-op shooter that escalates perfectly. Every run starts the same, ends differently.
The co-op loot shooter that defined a genre. Couch splitscreen, four-player madness, billions of guns.
Spiritual Left 4 Dead successor with card-based builds. Four-player co-op rewarding coordination.
Left 4 Dead meets Warhammer Fantasy. Melee-focused co-op with deep loot and class progression.
Co-op heist classic. Plan the job, execute the job, possibly blow everything up spectacularly.
Hero shooter with 40+ unique characters. Free to play, fast team composition gameplay.
Tense tactical 5v5 destruction shooter. Operator abilities and breach mechanics make every round unique.
The original battle royale. Slower pace, higher tension — one shot can genuinely end everything.
The defining open world. Still one of the most detailed cities ever created. GTA Online is a game within a game.
The most cinematic GTA. Niko Bellic's story remains one of gaming's most authentic human narratives.
A cultural phenomenon. Three cities, gang wars, iconic soundtrack — a world that felt genuinely massive in 2004.
80s Miami in neon. The soundtrack alone earns this a permanent spot on any all-time list.
The best open world storytelling in gaming history. Arthur Morgan's arc is genuinely moving. A technical marvel.
The Western epic. That ending is gaming's most emotionally devastating moment. Required playing for everyone.
Post-patches: one of the most atmospheric cities ever created. Night City rewards curiosity at every corner.
The benchmark for open world RPGs. Every side quest tells a complete story. Hundreds of hours, zero padding.
The most underrated open world game ever. Hong Kong, undercover cops, kung fu — absurdly, criminally good.
Mafia meets Sleeping Dogs in 80s Japan. Karaoke, real estate wars, one of gaming's best stories. Start here.
The most unique game ever made. Do not look anything up. A 22-minute time loop that becomes philosophy.
Survival on an alien ocean world. Beauty and terror in equal measure — atmosphere completely unmatched.
Rockstar's most overlooked gem. GTA-DNA in a school setting — funny, surprising, brilliantly designed.
The grappling hook sandbox. No game has ever matched the pure chaos-to-fun ratio of Just Cause 2.
Even bigger explosions plus a wingsuit. The physics sandbox pushed even further into glorious chaos.
The best Mafia remake. 1930s gangster story with genuine emotional weight — criminally underplayed.
Best story in the Mafia series. Vito Scaletta's rise is one of gaming's great character arcs.
Weakest in the series but great music and dark 60s atmosphere. Lincoln Clay's story carries real weight.
Renaissance Italy as a playground. Ezio Auditore is one of gaming's greatest protagonists.
Pirates plus Assassins equals perfection. Naval combat still holds up. Shanties that live in your head forever.
Paris is breathtaking. 4-player online co-op missions make this worth revisiting.
The best Far Cry. Vaas Montenegro is one of gaming's greatest villains. The island opens brilliantly.
Co-op campaign option, great Himalayan setting. Pagan Min is a compelling and memorable antagonist.
Secretly great. San Francisco sandbox with co-op that actually works well. Much better than the first.
Hacking open world set in Chicago. Grittier tone than the sequel, solid stealth systems throughout.
Totally transformed from its launch. Space sandbox with online multiplayer and deep base building.
Realistic hunting across stunning open worlds. Surprisingly meditative. Co-op with friends is excellent.
You are a shark. Open world ocean RPG. Absurd, fun, and surprisingly deep progression system.
Wuxia open world set in 10th century China. Stunning visuals and deep combat — promising newcomer.

GAMES.t: The Last of Us Part I
GAMES.d: The most cinematic game ever made. Joel and Ellie's journey is emotionally devastating and absolutely essential.
The greatest stealth sandbox ever created. Every level is a puzzle box with genuinely infinite solutions.
The best superhero game ever made. Gliding through Arkham City at night feels genuinely powerful.
Where the modern action game was born. The combat system it invented spawned an entire genre.
Batmobile-heavy finale with spectacular set pieces and a genuinely surprising story conclusion.
SCP meets brutalist architecture. Remedy's strangest and most atmospheric masterpiece to date.
Bioshock meets Deus Ex on a space station. Immersive sim freedom at its absolute finest.
Stealth or chaos — completely your choice. The Clockwork Mansion level alone justifies the purchase.
Cyberpunk RPG before Cyberpunk. The augmentation system and moral choices hold up perfectly today.
Best melee combat system in years. You age when you die — mastery feels genuinely and completely earned.
Titanfall 2 movement energy as a first-person parkour game. One hit kills. Flow state perfected.
FromSoftware's parry masterpiece. No build diversity — pure mechanical skill expression. Mastery feels incredible.
Most accessible Souls entry. Breathtaking boss design, summoning makes it approachable with a friend.
The genre-defining masterpiece. Death teaches, patience pays off, every shortcut discovered is deeply satisfying.

GAMES.t: Prince of Persia: Sands of Time
GAMES.d: Time-manipulation platforming pioneer. Still plays beautifully — proof that truly good design is timeless.
The stealth pinnacle. Couch co-op spy missions that remain uniquely tense even now in 2024.
More action-focused stealth. Online co-op prequel campaign is genuinely great and worth playing.
The Lara Croft reboot nobody expected to be this good. Cinematic, brutal, and emotionally strong.
Four-player couch co-op loot fest. The most approachable ARPG ever — drop in, destroy everything.
The ARPG origin. The atmosphere and item obsession loop remains genuinely unmatched decades later.
Interactive thriller with branching story. Perfect couch experience to watch unfold with someone else.
Neo-noir action platformer. One-hit kills, time manipulation, and a story that earns every single twist.
Isometric Zelda-like with a secret language to decipher. Gorgeous, mysterious, rewards genuine curiosity.
Card-based speedrun FPS. Unique mechanic, incredible style, replayable endlessly for perfectionists.
Roguelite third-person shooter with bullet-hell elements. Brutal, atmospheric, deeply satisfying to master.
The best RPG ever made — maybe the best game ever. Full couch co-op. Choices that genuinely matter.
Deepest CRPG in two decades. Fully playable in couch co-op. Every playthrough reveals new possibilities.
More novel than game. The most original RPG ever created — a detective story through a fractured psyche.
The greatest sci-fi trilogy in gaming. Your choices carry across three games. Shepard is truly yours.
Standalone masterpiece. Loyalty missions, squad building, and a suicide mission that feels genuinely earned.
The best Fallout. Faction politics, real consequences, the Mojave as a living breathing wasteland.
Most accessible Fallout. Base building, settlement management, and a huge amount of content to explore.
Tactical fantasy RPG with pauseable combat. Deep lore, genuine moral weight, six unique origin stories.
The open world RPG standard. Still absorbs hundreds of hours even fifteen years after release.
Yakuza goes turn-based and it's brilliant. Ichiban's story is unexpectedly heartfelt and genuinely hilarious.
Cult RPG with the best atmosphere in the genre. LA as a vampire feeding ground — dark, funny, brilliant.
Spiritual Fallout New Vegas successor. Satire, freedom, and a cast of genuinely memorable companions.
Solid open world RPG in the Wizarding World. The castle and surrounding grounds are lovingly recreated.
The best co-op game ever made. Every chapter reinvents itself completely. Non-optional purchase for any duo.
Prison break co-op adventure ending with an emotional gut-punch. One copy plays with two players online.
Voice-only co-op puzzles. You're separated — communication is literally the game mechanic. Atmospheric.
Ghost hunting co-op horror. Sessions become legendary stories. Best played late at night with headphones on.
Rock and Stone! Four dwarves vs alien bugs underground. The gold standard for co-op teamplay.
Cooking chaos that tests friendships. Perfect couch party game — frantic, funny, impossibly tense.
Floppy physics brawler. Zero skill ceiling, maximum chaos. The best accidental comedy game ever made.
The finest local multiplayer game. One arrow, one hit. Intensity and clarity in absolute perfect balance.
Play via phone — no controllers needed. Perfect for large groups. Quiplash and Drawful never get old.
Mario Party on PC with dark humor. Board game plus brutal minigames. Great for groups of 4 to 8.
Physics puzzle co-op that turns any level into improv comedy. Endlessly replayable with friends.
Social deduction at its finest. Accuse everyone, trust no one. Still creates memorable moments every session.
The original couch classic. Holy Hand Grenade, Ninja Rope, Holy Sheep of God — enough said.
The best modern Worms. Vehicles, crafting, couch and online play. Same chaotic magic preserved.
The best Lego game ever. Couch co-op through all six Star Wars films. Pure nostalgic joy.
Modern Lego title covering all nine films. Couch co-op throughout with a massive amount of content.
Lego Marvel classic. Couch co-op with a massive Marvel roster and great open hub world.
Heartfelt co-op platformer. Pull each other around with yarn. Gentle, beautiful, genuinely moving.
Carry furniture through chaotic levels. Physics-based couch mayhem — always ends in laughter.
Battle royale party game. Bright, chaotic, free to play. Great as a group viewing or playing experience.
Couch brawler with boomerangs. Simple to learn, surprisingly deep. Underrated party game gem.
One-on-one sword duel. Directional combat, instant death. Tension in its purest couch form.
Chaos multiplayer shooter with ducks. Couch party that never stops being funny to anyone.
Roguelite platformer with co-op. Couch play adds chaos and produces incredible emergent moments.
Couch co-op spaceship adventure. Charming, colorful, and genuinely requires teamwork to complete.
The definitive party racer. 4-player couch, gorgeous courses, perfect battle mode. Switch essential.
Best Mario Party in years — classic boards, great minigames, perfect for any group gathering.
4-player chaos platformer. Competitive co-op that brings out the worst in people in the absolute best way.
Metroidvania perfection. Hallownest is one of gaming's richest worlds with thousands of years of lore.
Perfect platforming meets genuine emotional storytelling. Mechanics and narrative inseparably intertwined.
The roguelite that defined the genre for a decade. Every build feels complete. Pure flow state.
The game that proved roguelites could have deep narrative. The story unfolds run by run organically.
Worthy successor, already exceptional in Early Access. New protagonist, expanded systems, same brilliant loop.
Pure movement, no other input needed. 4-player couch co-op. Shockingly addictive for how minimal it is.
1930s cartoon aesthetic with brutal difficulty. Two-player couch co-op boss rush that rewards mastery.
The best 2D platformer ever made. 4-player couch co-op, incredible music levels, creative design throughout.
2D Minecraft at full speed. Infinite content, deep crafting, couch splitscreen. Hundreds of hours guaranteed.
The sandbox that defined a generation. Couch splitscreen, online co-op — infinitely replayable.
Relaxing farm life RPG. Couch co-op with up to 4 players. The perfect chill session game with friends.
80s action movie pastiche co-op. Couch chaos, destructible environments, pure testosterone energy.
The best puzzle game ever made. Co-op campaign requires communication and trust — and is hilarious throughout.
The definitive city builder. Every system interconnects — traffic, economy, happiness. Endlessly deep.
Tense turn-based tactics. Every soldier you lose feels genuinely costly. Strategy at its most impactful.
Just one more turn for fifteen straight years. The perfect strategy sandbox — build, war, culture, or science.
Expanded systems with districts and governors. Online multiplayer makes diplomacy genuinely tense.
The best city-builder strategy hybrid. Industrial era supply chains that achieve genuine mathematical elegance.
Survival city builder where every decision carries moral weight. Do you sacrifice freedom for survival?
The Total War peak. Feudal Japan, samurai warfare — perfect balance between campaign and battle.
The e-sport colossus. Co-op missions make it accessible. Free to play now — no excuse not to try.
Best tower defense ever made. Deceptively deep, co-op available, hundreds of maps and challenges.
Dynasty sandbox in the medieval era. Every game tells a completely different emergent story.
Meditative driving through Europe. Online convoy mode makes long hauls surprisingly social.
Deep agricultural simulation. Co-op mode lets you run a farm with friends — strangely compelling.
Zoo management with the most detailed animal AI in any game. Creative and genuinely complex.
Business management sim with a very specific product. Surprisingly deep economic systems.
Infect the world. Unsettlingly realistic pandemic simulation — especially post-2020.
Blizzard's card game. Accessible entry point to the genre with deep competitive ladder options.
The crown of the NFS series. The Blacklist, police chases, and tuning in one perfect package.
Where tuner culture met gaming. Night racing, neon, hip hop — it invented an aesthetic for a generation.
Open world tuning culture simulator. Going deeper into every modification possible. The pinnacle.
Canyon racing and crew mechanics. A solid NFS entry carrying the Most Wanted torch forward.
Criterion's NFS. Best police chases in the series — Autolog online competition was revolutionary.
Crash physics never surpassed. Couch multiplayer that still induces screaming. A party game classic.
Open world arcade racing at its finest. Remastered and available now — every stunt feels incredible.
Open world racing in Mexico with dynamic seasons. Best driving feel outside pure simulation. Stunning.
UK setting with seasonal world changes. Still phenomenal — watching lakes freeze over in winter is magical.
The most underrated racing game ever made. Body-swapping mechanic is pure genius. Couch co-op available.
Defined the skating genre. Analog flick controls felt completely revolutionary. Still the best skating feel.
Co-op skating sessions and legendary physics glitches. The funniest unintentional comedy game ever made.
Perfect remaster of gaming's greatest skating games. Two minutes per level, still as good as ever.
The kart racer that rivals Mario Kart. Tighter drifting, harder tracks, incredible couch sessions.
Destruction racing couch classic. Ragdoll driver physics that absolutely never stop being funny.
Modern destruction derby with real physics. Most satisfying crashes in any racing game ever made.
Simulation racing benchmark. Massive mod community, endlessly deep for wheel and pad players alike.
Open world winter sports — skiing, snowboarding, wingsuit. Chill multiplayer exploration in the Alps.
Most realistic skateboarding physics system ever made. Steep learning curve, incredible replays.
Greatest platform fighter ever assembled. 80+ characters. Perfect at every skill level. Party essential.
Best MK ever. Cinematic story mode, incredible graphics, brutal fatalities. Couch versus perfection.
Best Street Fighter in decades. World Tour single-player mode, accessible controls, deep competitive system.
The deepest 3D fighting game. Gorgeous, accessible to newcomers, endlessly rewarding for veterans.
Best anime fighting game. Three vs three tag team chaos with art that looks just like the show.
Visually the most spectacular fighting game ever made. Unique characters, incredible soundtrack.
DC fighting game with a good story mode. Gear system adds progression casual players can enjoy.
The football simulation staple. Couch co-op and versus that works for any gathering, any skill level.
The perfect sports game. Football with rocket-powered cars. Infinite skill ceiling, free to play now.
Mini golf online party game. Somehow always funny. Perfect for groups wanting low-stakes competition.
The basketball simulation. MyCareer, couch versus, and online leagues. Best pick-up sports game.
Wrestling simulation with massive roster. Royal Rumble chaos mode is pure couch party gold.
The card-based roguelite that defined a genre. Build a deck, fight bosses, discover insane synergies. No stick clicks needed — Vita perfect.
Turn-based tactics in bite-sized missions. Every move is a puzzle. Perfect Vita game — pure strategic decisions, simple inputs.
Spaceship roguelite. Manage crew, upgrade systems, reach the flagship. Infinite replayability, controller-friendly.
The roguelite with the deepest item synergy system ever designed. Top-down dungeon crawler. Thousands of hours. Pure controller.
Gothic horror RPG. Manage stress, madness, and doomed heroes. Turn-based, atmospheric, and genuinely tense.
Beautiful pixel art action RPG. No dialogue, no tutorials — pure atmosphere and precise combat. No stick clicks. Vita ideal.
Perfect NES-style platformer with four full campaigns. Co-op in Shovel of Hope. Gold standard for indie platformers.
Dark Spanish Catholic horror Metroidvania. Brutal, beautiful, deeply atmospheric. No L3/R3 or shoulder buttons essential.
The most beautiful platformer ever made. Tight controls, emotional story, no complex combos. Vita-perfect.

GAMES.t: Ori and the Will of the Wisps
GAMES.d: Sequel that improves on everything. Fluid movement, expanded combat, stunning visuals. Fully Vita compatible.
Walk, run, glide to a mountain peak. Short, joyful, perfect. Under 2 hours. Simplest controls — Vita ideal.
Be a horrible goose. Puzzle-stealth with couch co-op option. Simplest controls possible. Guaranteed laughs.
Ferry the dead to the afterlife. Cozy management game that will make you cry. Couch co-op available.
Platformer as pure art. No death, no fail state — movement, color and stunning watercolor animation.
Playdead's masterpiece. Atmospheric puzzle-platformer with one of gaming's most haunting endings.
Black and white horror platformer. Minimalist, atmospheric, genuinely unsettling. Two-button controls — Vita perfect.
Supergiant's debut. Isometric action RPG with the best narrator in gaming. Beautiful hand-painted world.
Supergiant follow-up. Real-time and turn-based hybrid with stunning art and OST by Darren Korb.
Rhythm action album-game. Pure directional inputs, incredible electronic soundtrack, 40 minutes of pure bliss.
Detective mystery aboard a ghost ship. Deduce the fate of 60 crew members using only logic. Unique and brilliant.
Border checkpoint inspector simulator. Moral decisions under time pressure. Dystopian masterpiece of game design.
Puzzle game where the rules are objects you can push. Mind-bending, unique. No combat — pure logic.
The RPG where you do not have to kill anyone. Subverts every genre trope. Emotional, funny, one of a kind.
Toby Fox's follow-up to Undertale. Chapters 1-2 free. Just as clever, emotional, and surprising.
Action RPG set inside an MMO world. Deep environmental puzzles, fantastic combat, surprisingly great story.
Solo-developed Metroid tribute. Alien world, strange weapons, deep lore. Vita-perfect controls throughout.
Walking simulator about family deaths. Each chapter is a unique mechanic. Haunting, beautiful, unforgettable.
Wyoming wilderness mystery. First-person exploration, rich dialogue, stunning environments. Pure atmosphere.
Teen supernatural mystery with dialogue on the fly. Atmospheric radio mechanic, clever story, great characters.
Story-driven adventure about returning home and finding it changed. Witty, deeply sad, and very real.
Slay the Spire meets tower defense. Two-lane deck builder with incredible depth and replayability.
Run a shop by day, dungeon-crawl by night. Zelda-style action RPG with satisfying trading loop.
Vampire Survivors-style survival shooter with actual aiming. Short sessions, great builds — ideal for Vita travel.
Design subway networks in minimalist style. Calm, elegant, endlessly replayable. Ideal Vita commuter game.
Weird physics platformer with couch co-op. Funny, short, charming — perfect short session game.
Paint the world with a magic brush. Zelda-style adventure with emotional storytelling. No combat pressure.
Sequel that improves the original. More weapons, more worlds, same brutal atmosphere. Vita-friendly controls.
The defining PS1 platformer trilogy. Precise timing, memorable levels, iconic character. N.Sane Trilogy remaster brings it to modern platforms.
Open purple dragon collectathon. Vast colorful worlds, exploration-heavy, perfectly relaxed pace. Reignited Trilogy still excellent.
Hideo Kojima's masterpiece. Stealth action with a cinematic story decades ahead of its time. Foundation of the entire genre.
The RPG that defined a generation. Cloud, Sephiroth, Midgar — a story and world that changed gaming forever.
The most beloved Final Fantasy. Classic fantasy setting, memorable cast, emotional story. Available on PC and mobile too.
The survival horror classic. Leon and Claire, Raccoon City, the Spencer Mansion. Terrifying and brilliantly designed.
The Metroidvania that defined the genre. Richter, Alucard, the inverted castle — a perfect 2D action RPG.
The fighting game peak of the PS1 era. Fluid combat, massive roster, still playable and fun in couch sessions.
The definitive skateboarding game of its era. Two-minute sessions, perfect feel, iconic soundtrack.
The kart racer that rivaled Mario Kart on PS1. Tight controls, personality-packed, still holds up beautifully.
The horror game that understood psychological fear. Foggy town, radio static, monsters from your own mind.
Over 650 cars on PS1. The simulation racing bible of its era — absurdly deep for the hardware.
Undead knight saves the kingdom. Quirky British humor, great atmosphere, PS1 cult classic with a great remake.
Precursor to GTA driving gameplay. Chase scenes, free roaming cities, stunt mode — ahead of its time.
16 colossi, no other enemies. A meditation on violence, loss, and forbidden resurrection. Gaming as art, still unmatched.
Kratos' origin. Greek mythology, brutal combat, insane set pieces for 2005 hardware. Defined a generation of action games.
Bigger, better, more spectacular. Best PS2 action game ever made. The Colossus of Rhodes intro remains jaw-dropping.
Boy holds girl's hand through a castle. One of gaming's most atmospheric experiences. Companion AI before it existed.
Play as the sun goddess Amaterasu as a white wolf. Zelda-like with watercolor aesthetic that still looks stunning today.
Disney meets Final Fantasy in a surprisingly deep action RPG. Emotional story, brilliant world-hopping concept.
The sequel that perfected the combat system. More complex, more emotional, more Disney worlds — peak Kingdom Hearts.
Seamless open world platformer on PS2. No loading screens, sprawling landscape, precise jumping. Naughty Dog at their best.
Platformer meets third-person shooter. Ridiculous arsenal of weapons, great humor, massive worlds for PS2 hardware.
The peak of the PS2 Ratchet games. More weapons, online multiplayer, refined everything. A perfect sequel.

GAMES.t: Sly Cooper and the Thievius Raccoonus
GAMES.d: Stylish cel-shaded stealth platformer. Thief raccoon with a gang. Charming, tight, criminally underrated.
The definitive DBZ fighting game with 150+ characters. Couch battles that never get old. Maximum anime chaos.
The best snowboarding game ever made. One connected mountain, incredible soundtrack, perfect arcade feel.
GTA but Simpsons. Springfield fully realized, hilarious writing, best licensed game ever made.

GAMES.t: Metal Gear Solid 2: Sons of Liberty
GAMES.d: The tanker and Big Shell. Raiden's story. Kojima's most postmodern game — about information, identity, and control.
Cold War jungle espionage. Big Boss origin. The Cobra Unit, The Boss ending — one of gaming's greatest stories.
Reinvented the action game. Over-the-shoulder camera, Ganados, chainsaw man, regenerators — still holds up perfectly.
The greatest survival horror game ever made. Pyramid Head, guilt, grief — psychological horror as high art.
Roll a sticky ball to pick up everything. Completely original concept, joyful soundtrack, impossible to describe.
First Tony Hawk with a story mode. Open world cities, create-a-skater, still one of the best in the series.
Night racing tuner culture at its PS2 peak. Underground 1 and 2 defined a generation of racing fans.
Open world street racing with licensed vehicles and DUB Magazine culture. Best in the Midnight Club series.
Rockstar's most overlooked masterpiece. Navigate school cliques, fight bullies, romance classmates — GTA in a school.
The stylish action peak of the PS2 era. Dante's origin, combat depth, insane boss fights — still the gold standard.
Dark vehicular combat game. Unhinged characters, brutal gameplay, couch multiplayer carnage.
Traffic checking added to the Burnout formula. Couch racing chaos at its finest.
Geo-Mod engine lets you blow up everything. Destructible environments in 2002 — impressive and still fun.
The original Souls game. Brutally hard, atmospheric, landmark in game design. Where FromSoftware's legacy began.
The game that redefined cinematic action. Train sequence, Nepal, the monastery — still one of gaming's greatest adventures.
Greek mythology conclusion. Zeus as final boss, Kratos' rage at full force. PS3 showcase title that still looks incredible.
Old Snake's final mission. Longest cutscenes in gaming, but the gameplay and conclusion hit incredibly hard.

GAMES.t: Ni no Kuni: Wrath of the White Witch
GAMES.d: Studio Ghibli animated JRPG. Oliver's journey to save his mother — the most beautiful RPG on PS3.
Watercolor-style WWII tactical RPG. Unique blend of real-time movement and turn-based combat. Hidden gem.
Walk across a desert to a glowing mountain with a stranger. No words, no combat. One of gaming's most moving experiences.
Play, create, share. Sackboy platformer with a full level editor. 4-player couch co-op childhood staple.
Expanded creation tools and a great story mode. The definitive LBP experience.
Interactive noir thriller. Four characters, one killer, branching story. Perfect couch watch-together experience.
Electricity-powered open world superhero game. Cole MacGrath in Empire City — moral choices shape your powers.
Post-apocalyptic Washington DC. VATS, exploration, radio — the game that made open world RPGs mainstream.
The best Fallout. Faction politics, real consequences, the Mojave wasteland. Obsidian's masterpiece.
GTA but completely unhinged. Superpowers, wrestling matches, airdrops — the most chaotic open world game ever.
Third-person shooter that deconstructs shooter violence. Heart of Darkness in Dubai. Haunting and important.
Pixel voxel art Zelda homage. Create your own hero sprite, explore dungeons — charming love letter to retro gaming.
Japanese theatrical platformer. Puppets on a stage, heads as power-ups, stunning visual design. Forgotten masterpiece.
Gorgeous 2D platformer with 4-player couch co-op. UbiArt engine at its most beautiful. Pure platforming joy.

GAMES.t: God of War: Chains of Olympus
GAMES.d: Full God of War experience on PSP. Prequel to the PS2 games, incredible for handheld hardware.

GAMES.t: God of War: Ghost of Sparta
GAMES.d: Even better than Chains of Olympus. Deimos' story, emotional beats, best PSP action game ever made.
Full GTA experience on handheld. Liberty City stories set before GTA III — impressively faithful port.
Empire building mechanic added to Vice City. One of the best PSP open world games ever made.
Full Persona 3 on handheld with new female protagonist route. JRPG masterpiece — available now on modern platforms.
Zack Fair's story before FFVII. Real-time combat, emotional ending — essential for any Final Fantasy fan.
The game that made Monster Hunter. Hunt monsters, craft gear, repeat forever. Addictive loop that never gets old.
Rhythm-based strategy with tribal warriors. Drum commands, impossible to describe, impossible to put down.
Tilt to roll colorful blobs through a world. Completely original concept, joyful music, Vita-perfect controls.
Big Boss builds Mother Base. Co-op missions, base management, emotional conclusion to the Cold War MGS saga.
Fighting game with every Final Fantasy hero and villain. 3D arena battles, deep RPG leveling, fan service peak.
Music-rhythm puzzle game. Falling blocks matched to electronic music. Created by Tetsuya Mizuguchi — still incredible.
Anti-gravity racing at its best. Incredible techno soundtrack, tight controls, beautiful for PSP hardware.
Full Tekken 5 on a handheld. Ad-hoc multiplayer, ghost battles, the best PSP fighting game.
The definitive version of the best JRPG ever made. Social links, dungeon crawling, Inaba — perfect in every way.
Shift gravity to fall in any direction. Kat is one of gaming's best characters. Unique traversal on Vita.
Full Uncharted experience on Vita. Incredible tech showcase for the hardware on launch.
The best FPS ever made on a handheld. Console-quality graphics and gameplay on Vita hardware.
Paper craft world that uses every Vita feature. Rear touchpad, front camera — charming and completely original.
Post-apocalyptic action RPG. Sentenced prisoners, monster hunts, chain mechanics — co-op on Vita via ad-hoc.
Visual novel murder mystery. Trapped students, killing game, Monokuma — completely gripping from start to finish.
Sequel that improves on every aspect. New island, new cast, even more shocking twists. Now available on PC too.
Anti-gravity racing prequel. Beautiful visuals for Vita hardware, tight controls, incredible soundtrack.
Top-down ultra-violent action puzzle. Plan, execute, die, repeat. Psychedelic, brutal, unforgettable.
2D skateboarding roguelite. One-button trick system with incredible depth. Built for handheld play.
Shoot-em-up combined with foot sections. Teleporting mechanic, incredible speed, best Vita exclusive.
Japanese watercolor art action game. Two characters, hundreds of swords, gorgeous 2D combat.
Side-scrolling beat-em-up with RPG depth. 6 classes, beautiful painted art style, co-op dungeons.
Platformer where everything you touch creates music. Deadmau5 and Beck contributed levels — unique experience.
The definitive Pokemon experience. Two regions, 16 gyms, Pokemon following you — the peak of the series.
Fourth generation Pokemon. Sinnoh region, the Underground, Contests — beloved generation with amazing music.
The most story-driven mainline Pokemon game. N's arc, Team Plasma, the best narrative in the franchise.
First portable Mario Kart with proper online play. Retro tracks, mission mode — still one of the best in the series.
2D Mario returned after 20 years. Fresh, tight, nostalgic — launched a new era of 2D platformers.
Best Mario & Luigi game. Play inside Bowser's body and outside simultaneously. Hilarious, creative writing.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Phantom Hourglass
GAMES.d: Wind Waker sequel on DS. Touchscreen controls work beautifully. The Temple of the Ocean King is brilliant.
Best JRPG ever ported to DS. Time travel epic with multiple endings — if you haven't played this, stop everything.
Best DS Metroidvania. Soul system lets you absorb enemy powers. Tight, deep, endlessly replayable.
Western rhythm game with touching stories. Tap to the beat, save people's lives. One of DS's greatest games.
Turn-based military strategy at its finest. CO powers, dual screen battles, deep and accessible.
First portable Animal Crossing. Real-time clock, online trading — endlessly relaxing daily life sim.

GAMES.t: Professor Layton and the Curious Village
GAMES.d: Puzzle adventure with an English village mystery. Hundreds of puzzles, charming story, great music.
Objection! Defense lawyer visual novel. Five cases, memorable characters, iconic writing. Still brilliant today.
First 3D Pokemon. Kalos region, mega evolutions, character customization — fresh and beautifully presented.
Hawaii-inspired Pokemon. Island trials instead of gyms, great characters, emotional story for the series.
The definitive Hoenn remakes. Soaring on Latias, Delta Episode in space — best Pokemon remakes ever made.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time 3D
GAMES.d: The greatest game ever made, now perfectly playable on handheld. 3D makes sense here — masterpiece remastered.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Majora's Mask 3D
GAMES.d: The darkest and most complex Zelda. Three-day cycle, 72 hours, the moon falls — brilliant and haunting.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: A Link Between Worlds
GAMES.d: Top-down Zelda set in A Link to the Past's Hyrule. Wall-merge mechanic is genius. The best new Zelda in years.
3D Mario designed for handheld. Tanooki suit returns, 3D effect actually adds depth, hundreds of clever levels.
Kart customization and gliders added. Online multiplayer, great track selection — essential 3DS title.
The Fire Emblem that saved the series. Accessible, emotional, deep tactics. Chrom and Lucina's story is brilliant.
You are the mayor. Most content of any AC game, deep customization, daily rituals — the best handheld AC.
Classic JRPG in a gorgeous art style. Brave and Default mechanics reinvent turn-based combat. Love letter to FF.
The peak of handheld Monster Hunter. Mounting system, insect glaive, deep co-op — the gold standard.
Masahiro Sakurai's 3DS masterpiece. Air battles, ground sections, 25 chapters of pure creativity and humor.
Multiple haunted mansions for Luigi to explore. Poltergust vacuum, clever puzzles, co-op ScareScraper mode.
Perfect NES homage with four full campaigns. Beautifully adapted for 3DS handheld play.
Metroidvania mining game. Dig deeper, find upgrades, discover the story. Perfect handheld loop.
Metroid II remake. Melee counter added to the formula, atmospheric, one of the last great 3DS titles.
The Ace Attorney revival. Great cases, Athena Cykes, emotional finale — essential for visual novel fans.
3D platformer in space. Gravity-bending levels, orchestral soundtrack, the most creative Mario ever made.
More galaxies, more ideas, Yoshi added. Some argue it's even better than the first — top 5 games ever made.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess
GAMES.d: Dark, mature Zelda. Wolf Link, Midna, Hyrule in shadow — the most epic Zelda scope. Gorgeous HD remaster exists.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Skyward Sword
GAMES.d: Zelda's origin story. Motion controls for sword combat, Groose, emotional conclusion to the timeline.
The Subspace Emissary adventure plus legendary couch multiplayer. Snake, Sonic added. A generation's party game.
Bikes, 12-player online, Wii Wheel — the best-selling Mario Kart for years. Grumble Volcano still hits hard.
Three Metroid Prime games in one. First-person Metroid with exploration and atmosphere unlike anything else.
DKC revival with Retro Studios. Hard as nails, gorgeous, 2-player couch co-op — one of the best 2D platformers.
The definitive party game of the Wii era. Swordplay, basketball, table tennis — MotionPlus made this perfect.
Gorgeous 2D platformer with 4-player couch co-op. UbiArt engine at its most joyful and creative.
Hironobu Sakaguchi's Wii RPG. Online multiplayer, real-time combat, emotional story. Rare Western release.
Travis Touchdown ranks up by assassinating the top 10. Stylish, bizarre, Suda51 at his most original.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild (Wii U)
GAMES.d: Open world Zelda that reinvented the series. Physics engine, cooking, climbing — the definitive BOTW version for collectors.
Anti-gravity tracks, incredible visuals, 200cc mode. The definitive Mario Kart before Deluxe refined it further.

GAMES.t: Super Smash Bros. for Wii U
GAMES.d: Huge roster, custom movesets, 8-player smash. The most ambitious Smash before Ultimate.
Nintendo's original shooter. Ink-based territory control, original concept — proof Nintendo can do shooters.
Best action game ever made. Witch Time, hair weapons, hair portals — technical mastery beyond any other game.

GAMES.t: Donkey Kong Country: Tropical Freeze (Wii U)
GAMES.d: Retro Studios' masterpiece. Beautifully crafted levels, David Wise soundtrack, challenging and gorgeous.
Alien planet strategy game. Beautifully detailed world, 3-captain system, co-op mission mode.
Create and share Mario levels. The most creative Nintendo game ever released. Gamepad is perfect for building.
Zelda meets Dynasty Warriors. Mow down thousands of enemies as Zelda characters — couch co-op chaos.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
GAMES.d: Open world Zelda that reinvented the series. Physics-based puzzles, cooking, emergent moments — paradigm shift.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
GAMES.d: Built on BOTW's world with Ultrahand and Fuse. Sky islands, Depths, building vehicles — sequel that surpasses original.
Mario captures enemies with his hat. New Donk City, Moon Kingdom, incredible density of ideas per level.
Deserted island daily life. Launched during COVID lockdowns and became cultural phenomenon. Co-op visiting islands.
Expanded on the original with Salmon Run co-op mode. Ink turf wars, fresh fashion, great multiplayer.
New maps, new weapons, Tableturf card game. Best Salmon Run yet — the definitive Splatoon experience.
Teach students at a military academy then go to war. Four routes, deep relationships, best strategy game on Switch.
PlatinumGames action game with Legions tethered to you. Unique dual-combat system, incredible style and pace.
2D Metroid masterpiece. EMMI enemies create pure terror, movement system the best in any 2D Metroid ever.
First fully 3D Kirby. Post-apocalyptic world, Mouthful Mode, 2-player co-op — charming and surprisingly deep.
Ghost-hunting in a hotel. Gooigi enables co-op, floor-by-floor exploration, best Luigi's Mansion ever made.
XCOM meets Mario — should be terrible, is brilliant. Turn-based tactics with Mario humor. Co-op campaign included.
Emotional JRPG epic. Six characters from opposing armies, colony system, over 100 hours — best in the series.
Open world Pokemon in feudal Sinnoh. Catch without battling, seamless world, finally a different Pokemon formula.
Multiverse Bayonetta with Viola added as new character. Summoning demons in real-time combat — chaotic and spectacular.
Most accessible Pikmin. New dog companion Oatchi, time rewind, Dandori challenges — great entry point to the series.
Team Cherry's masterpiece on Switch. Portable Metroidvania perfection — one of the best games ever made.
The best roguelite on Switch. Portable roguelite in handheld mode is the perfect way to play.
Perfect platformer on Switch. Assist mode makes it accessible to all. Emotional narrative hits differently portable.
Farming life RPG on Switch is the ultimate portable comfort game. Co-op added makes it even better.
Bros attacks in the dream world. Giant battles on the bottom screen — the most creative Mario & Luigi entry.
Two RPG universes collide. Paper Mario meets Mario & Luigi. Trio attacks, great humor, solid entry.
Kirby platformer with hypernova power. Beautiful 3D depth effects, tight levels, fantastic boss fights.
Kirby absorbs mechs. Mechanical aesthetic twist on the formula — arguably the best 3DS Kirby game.
Import your Miis and watch them live their bizarre lives. Zero gameplay, infinite entertainment — social sim chaos.
Action RPG where you choose one of 12 life classes. Blacksmith, mage, paladin — deep and endlessly charming.
Harvest Moon meets dungeon crawling. Farm, fight, fall in love. One of the best relaxation RPGs ever made.
Original Harvest Moon rebranded. Farming, shipping crops, building relationships — the definitive 3DS farm sim.
First-person dungeon RPG where you draw your own map. Deep class customization, punishing but rewarding.
Post-apocalyptic demon collection RPG. Press-turn system, alignment choices, dark tone. Gateway SMT game.

GAMES.t: Persona Q: Shadow of the Labyrinth
GAMES.d: Persona meets Etrian Odyssey dungeon crawling. P3 and P4 casts together — fan service done right.
The hardest Fire Emblem. No grinding, brutal maps, demands strategic mastery. For veterans.

GAMES.t: Fire Emblem Echoes: Shadows of Valentia
GAMES.d: Gaiden remake with full voice acting. Dungeon exploration, unique class system, emotional story.
Sequel to Bravely Default. Improved job system, great new characters, more of what made the first brilliant.
Final Fantasy music rhythm game. Tap along to 70+ iconic tracks. One of the best music games ever made.
200+ songs, versus mode added. The definitive Theatrhythm experience.
Two iconic puzzle series crossover. Courtroom meets Laytonesque logic — a collaboration that actually works perfectly.

GAMES.t: Professor Layton and the Azran Legacy
GAMES.d: Final chapter of the original Layton trilogy. Global adventure, emotional conclusion to the Hershel Layton saga.
Possess objects to prevent deaths. Shu Takumi's other masterpiece. Unique mechanic, brilliant story, unforgettable ending.
Escape rooms plus branching story. Prisoner's dilemma with 9 characters. One of the best visual novel games ever.

GAMES.t: Nine Hours, Nine Persons, Nine Doors
GAMES.d: Ship escape room visual novel. 9 people, 9 hours, 9 doors. Tense, clever, multiple endings, brilliant writing.
Time-travel tactical RPG. Manipulate timelines to find the true history. Deep combat, emotional story.
Full Xenoblade Chronicles on New 3DS. The JRPG epic fully portable — incredible for the hardware.
Hunter Arts and Styles added. Celebration of the series history. Co-op hunting at its most varied.

GAMES.t: Dragon Quest VII: Fragments of the Forgotten Past
GAMES.d: Longest JRPG ever made. Reconstructing a shattered world fragment by fragment. 200+ hours of content.

GAMES.t: Dragon Quest VIII: Journey of the Cursed King
GAMES.d: Best Dragon Quest. Hero on a quest to break a curse. Voice acting, photography mechanic, gorgeous art style.
Jill Valentine on a ship. Scan mode, tight corridors, genuinely scary. The best Resident Evil on handheld.
Perfect remake of the N64 classic. Barrel rolls, Lylat system, Peppy's advice — do a barrel roll.
Minimalist box puzzle platformer. Create chains of boxes to solve increasingly clever puzzles. Genius simplicity.
Flip gravity instead of jumping. C418-esque chiptune soundtrack, 20 minutes or 3 hours depending on skill.
Best Shantae game. Genie half lost but pirate gear gained. Tight metroidvania with incredible charm and music.
Turn-based tactics with bullet ricocheting. Steam robots in space. More fun than it has any right to be.
Pull and push blocks to climb to the top. Nintendo puzzle genius at its most elegant. Perfect for handheld.

GAMES.t: Castlevania: Lords of Shadow - Mirror of Fate
GAMES.d: Gothic Castlevania with three playable characters across generations. Beautiful and atmospheric.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Tri Force Heroes
GAMES.d: Three-player co-op Zelda. Stack on each other's heads, solve puzzles, sew outfits — unusual but brilliant.
P3, P4, and P5 casts all together. Movie world dungeons, great character interactions, fan service peak.
Victorian London lawyer mysteries. Two full games, incredible characters, the best writing in the AA series.
Victorian lawyer game spanning two full titles. Ryunosuke Naruhodo in London — the best Ace Attorney ever made.
The dark Persona. Rumors become real, Hitler awakens, the world ends. More mature than P3/P4 — essential.
Joan of Arc as a tactical JRPG. Beautiful anime art, deep strategy, one of PSP's most overlooked gems.
Tactical RPG with infinite depth. Level 9999, Item World, billions of stat points. Best PSP strategy game.

GAMES.t: Tactics Ogre: Let Us Cling Together
GAMES.d: The definitive version of one of strategy gaming's greatest works. Choices that matter, systems that reward.

GAMES.t: Final Fantasy Tactics: The War of the Lions
GAMES.d: The definitive FFT. Updated sprites, new jobs, full voice acting. Political fantasy tactical RPG masterpiece.

GAMES.t: Final Fantasy IV: The Complete Collection
GAMES.d: FFIV plus all sequels/prequels in one package. The complete Cecil Harvey saga beautifully remastered.
Three protagonists, three stories, one prequel to KH1. Command Board, D-Links, reveals the true lore of the series.
Rondo of Blood remake plus unlockable originals. The crown jewel of PSP platformers.
The Ys that converts everyone. Intense boss fights, incredible rock soundtrack, tight action RPG perfection.
Party-based Ys. First team combat in the series, great story, and one of the best soundtracks on PSP.
The origins remastered beautifully. Bump combat, incredible music — essential for JRPG history fans.
Full Valkyria Chronicles on PSP. Military academy setting, excellent squad tactics, emotional story.
Drug trade GTA with dual-stick setup. Top-down perspective, mature content, full open world on a handheld.
Top-down shooter with cover mechanics. One of the best PSP exclusives — technically and gameplay-wise.
Save the world in 30 seconds. Parodies every RPG trope at breakneck speed. Multiple game modes, pure genius.
Bigger and better Patapon. More gods, more enemies, multiplayer added. The rhythm-strategy peak.
Final Patapon. Hero units, online multiplayer, deeper build system — series conclusion done right.
More levels, more blobs, more songs. The joyful tilt-platformer expanded and refined.
Anti-gravity racing sequel. New track editor, Zone mode, incredible electronic music — must-have PSP racer.
Complete SF Alpha 3 with extra characters. The portable fighting game for PSP ad-hoc battles.
Open world LA street racing on PSP. Surprisingly faithful to the console version — best PSP racer.
800+ cars, no career mode but pure driving. The tech showcase PSP game that proved handheld sim racing.
The definitive Ridge Racer handheld. Drift mechanics, massive track count, incredible soundtrack.
Roll up islands. Same wonderful Katamari DNA perfectly adapted for PSP handheld play.
Full Worms experience on PSP. Ad-hoc multiplayer means four players on the same platform — couch classic.
Full Ratchet & Clank on PSP. All the weapons and gadgets you love in a portable package.
Ottsel exterminator spin-off. One of the best PSP platformers — more than just a spin-off filler.
All modes from Burnout 1-3 in a handheld package. Ad-hoc multiplayer crash mode. Essential early PSP game.
The best handheld third-person shooter before Killzone Mercenary. Underwater levels, incredible for PSP.
Jump between cars at high speed to fight criminals. Pure arcade action unlike anything else on PSP.
Travis climbs the assassin rankings again. More bosses, better pacing, incredible metal soundtrack.

GAMES.t: Zack & Wiki: Quest for Barbaros' Treasure
GAMES.d: Point-and-click adventure using Wii Remote. Most creative motion control game ever made. Criminally overlooked.
Pikmin meets RPG as a child king conquers the world. Charming art, deep systems, surprising emotional depth.
Watercolor Zelda-like with Celestial Brush painting mechanics. Wii remote as paintbrush is the definitive version.
Little Mac returns. Pattern recognition boss fights with Wii motion option. Perfect sequel to the NES classic.
Rail shooter by Treasure. Frantic bullet-dodging, screen-filling bosses, one of the best Wii exclusives.
2.5D platformer remake. Dreamworld aesthetic, wind bullet mechanic, one of gaming's most emotional endings.
4-player couch Kirby platformer. Super abilities, UFO copy power, best multiplayer Kirby on Wii.
Yarn aesthetic Kirby. Whip and wrap enemies, incredibly charming visual style, 2-player couch co-op.
Flip between 2D and 3D dimensions. Meta humor, great writing, different from standard Paper Mario formula.
Side-scrolling Japanese action with Vanillaware's gorgeous painted art. Two samurai stories, hundreds of swords.
Marvel vs Capcom style crossover with anime characters. Never released in many regions — rare and incredible.
MH on Wii with online lobbies. Underwater combat added, great community, defined a generation of MH fans.
Dark Zelda-like dungeon crawler. Feed your cursed girlfriend monster flesh. Operation Rainfall cult classic.
Sequel to Path of Radiance. Ike's story continues on Wii. Harder than almost any other FE — rewarding mastery.
Original Pikmin with pointer controls. The Wii remote makes the alien garden strategy more precise and fun.
Best Pikmin game fully updated for Wii. Caves, two Pikmin captains, collect treasure — no time limit freedom.
Sonic's best 3D game. Wisps add power-ups, tight level design, genuinely funny writing. The Sonic revival.
All six Bit.Trip games. Commander Video's journey across rhythm-based genres — WiiWare's greatest achievement.
Build structures with living goo balls. Physics-based puzzle indie masterpiece — one of WiiWare's best.
Roll a blob to paint a city. Cheerful, colorful, great motion controls — unique platformer concept.
Greatest Wii RPG. Bionis and Mechonis, real-time combat, incredible scope. 100+ hour epic.
Traditional JRPG on Wii. Beautiful art, solid turn-based combat, underrated gem.
Steven Spielberg produced party physics game. Throw things at blocks. Surprisingly deep and fun.
DK platformer originally with bongo controls, updated for Wii remote. Stylish and satisfying.
Launch Wii U Mario with 4-player couch co-op. Crisp HD Mario visuals for the first time. Classic platforming.
Toad explores dioramas you can rotate. Puzzle platformer with creative use of the Wii U gamepad camera.
Yarn-crafted Yoshi adventure. Amiibo fabric designs, 2-player couch co-op, gorgeous tactile aesthetic.
Draw lines on the gamepad for Kirby to follow. Claymation art style, co-op with helpers, unique and charming.
Card-based Paper Mario. Colorless Prism Island mystery, great humor and writing — underrated in the series.
Unite 100 heroes with drawing gestures. Hideki Kamiya's most creative game. Chaotic, stylish, brilliant.
Survival horror with permadeath. Gamepad as scanner and inventory — best use of Wii U's unique controller.
Watercolor fairy tale RPG. Aurora journeys through Lemuria in verse. Beautiful, short, deeply moving.
3-player co-op spaceship puzzle game. Each player controls different ship systems. Best Wii U indie game.
F-Zero successor on Wii U. Phase-switching mechanic, incredible speed, competitive local multiplayer.
Remixed NES challenges in microgame format. Brilliant way to revisit classics with new twists.
Pokemon fighting game by Tekken studio. Machamp vs Charizard in a real fighter — surprisingly deep.

GAMES.t: Fatal Frame: Maiden of Black Water
GAMES.d: Horror game using gamepad as Camera Obscura. Photograph ghosts to defeat them on haunted Mt. Hikami.
12 attraction theme park. Asymmetric multiplayer using gamepad uniquely. The best Wii U party game.
Bowser Party mode uses gamepad vs players. Unique asymmetric party gameplay format.
9-player couch multiplayer platformer. Platforms disappear as background color changes — chaotic fun.
Best version with full GamePad features. Perfect NES-style platformer on the Wii U.
Original Wii U version with Murphy GamePad touch controls. The way the game was meant to be played.
Alien planet colonization RPG with mechs. Open world bigger than any Nintendo game, 300+ hours — criminally overlooked.
The best 2D Mario in decades. Wonder Flowers transform each course completely. 4-player couch co-op.
Ring-based battle system and open world. Best paper art design in the series, great humor and heart.
Remake of the best Paper Mario ever made. Shadow Sirens, X-Nauts, Crystal Stars — a love letter to fans.
Front-and-back crafting world. Every level has hidden items on both sides. Adorable 2-player co-op.

GAMES.t: Kirby's Return to Dream Land Deluxe
GAMES.d: Remaster of the best Wii Kirby. New epilogue featuring Magolor. 4-player couch co-op perfected.
Funky Mode makes it accessible. Still the hardest and most beautiful 2D platformer on Switch.
Best Pikmin game fully enhanced. 2-player co-op story, all DLC, more content than any other version.
The greatest JRPG finally truly portable. Future Connected epilogue added. The definitive version.
Alrest and the World Tree. Blade gacha system, emotional story, Mythra and Pyra — divisive but brilliant.
One of the best games ever made, now in 4K. Dual-stick controls option, incredible atmosphere preserved.

GAMES.t: Bayonetta Origins: Cereza and the Lost Demon
GAMES.d: Young Bayonetta fairy tale. Dual-control system, storybook art, completely different from the main series.
Shin Megami Tensei meets Fire Emblem in J-pop Tokyo. Unique, stylish, underrated JRPG gem.
Post-apocalyptic Tokyo SMT. Demon fusion, press-turn system, open world — the first true Switch JRPG epic.
The definitive version of the best JRPG of the decade. Portable Persona 5 is a dream come true.
P4G finally on Switch. Inaba, Junes, the Truth — portable P4G is the best way to play.
Full remake of Persona 3. New animations, reworked combat, new scenes — the definitive Persona 3.

GAMES.t: Dragon Quest XI S: Echoes of an Elusive Age
GAMES.d: The best traditional JRPG in 20 years. Symphonic soundtrack, Orchestral mode, 2D retro mode — incredible value.
New world, same Brave-Default mechanic. Job system, turn-based mastery, proper difficulty curves.
HD-2D visuals debuted here. Eight characters, eight stories, beautiful music by Yasunori Nishiki.
Everything improved. Stories connect better, Latent Powers, day-night interactions — surpasses the original.
War of three nations. Every vote matters, multiple endings, the most morally complex Switch game.

GAMES.t: Ni no Kuni: Wrath of the White Witch (Switch)
GAMES.d: Studio Ghibli JRPG now portable. Oliver saves his mother across a parallel world — emotional and beautiful.
Full Witcher 3 on Switch. Portable Geralt in Novigrad and Skellige — technical miracle on handheld hardware.
Full rip-and-tear DOOM on Switch. Handheld mode surprisingly solid. Portable demon slaying done right.
Wirebugs and Palamutes. Fluid vertical movement, Rampage mode, best-looking Switch Monster Hunter.
Massive expansion. Elgado outpost, new flagship monsters, Silkbind Attacks — doubles the base game.
Phoenix Wright's first three cases remastered. HD art, touch controls, objection scenes never get old.
13 teenagers, 13 storylines, one mystery. Vanillaware's most ambitious game — visual novel plus RTS mashup.
2B, 9S, A2. Action RPG with multiple playthroughs revealing the truth. Soundtrack and story are legendary.

GAMES.t: The World Ends With You Final Remix
GAMES.d: Shibuya life-or-death game. Dual-screen combat on one screen, incredible music, unique fashion system.
Crypt of the Necrodancer meets Zelda. Move to the beat, explore Hyrule in roguelite rhythm action.
The most original RPG ever made, now portable. Play detective Harrier Du Bois in your pocket.
Deepest CRPG on Switch. Portable turn-based fantasy RPG with full co-op support.
Cut your friends into shapes to solve puzzles. The game that sold the Switch concept. 2-4 player couch.
Both Overcooked games plus DLC. The definitive party cooking experience with online co-op added.
Best co-op game ever on Switch. Every level reinvents itself. Portable couples' game night essential.
Crow collects souls as a reaper. Isometric action with Soulslike elements. Stunning art, tight combat.
Golf RPG on Switch eShop. Story about a golfer trying to reclaim his glory. Charming, surprising, deep.
Pixel art action RPG. John and Sam travel a dying world. Pixar-like storytelling in 16-bit aesthetic.
Roguelite dungeon shooter. Chaotic item interactions, incredible variety — every run completely different.
99-player F-Zero battle royale. Use Spin Attacks to charge boost, race the original tracks with a twist.
The legendary N64 shooter back on Switch. Split-screen multiplayer, restored Bond experience — nostalgia peak.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Link's Awakening (Switch)
GAMES.d: Gorgeous toy-box remake of the Game Boy classic. Koholint Island, Dreaming, dungeons — all perfected.

GAMES.t: The Legend of Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom
GAMES.d: First Zelda game with Zelda as protagonist. Echo system creates objects to solve puzzles — fresh Zelda formula.
Best Valkyria Chronicles since the original. Squad 7 on Switch, campaign and skirmish modes, watercolor combat.
Detective thriller by Kotaro Uchikoshi. Enter dreams to solve murders. Mystery visual novel with twists.
999 and VLR in one package. Escape room horror visual novels that redefine what games can be.
All three Danganronpa games in one. Murder mystery killing games — gripping from first case to last.
Classic Mario Party boards return. Gem of the Mario Party series with Joy-Con motion minigames.
Aggressive arcade football. Hyper Strikes, gear upgrades, 4v4 couch chaos — most fun Mario sports.
Wii Sports for the modern era. Soccer, tennis, bowling, volleyball — motion controls perfected.
Extendable arms fighting game. Motion or traditional controls, 15 fighters, DNA Lab party mode.
Parkour zombie survival in an open world. Night mechanics create genuine tension. Co-op makes it legendary.
Bigger city, expanded parkour, choices that reshape the world. Co-op throughout the campaign.
Post-apocalyptic car combat open world. Underrated Avalanche masterpiece — Fury Road as a video game.
Alien invasion, Matrix-style superpowers in Steelport. Even more absurd than The Third.
Consume enemies to gain powers. New York open world with satisfying combat power progression.
Neon and smoke powers in Seattle. Most visually impressive PS4 launch title. Great moral choices.
Viking survival crafting in a procedural Norse world. Co-op with friends is magical and deep.
Plane crash survivor vs cannibal mutants. Build your shelter, explore caves, survive the island.
Sequel with AI companion Kelvin. More building, bigger world, even more terrifying natives.
Amazon rainforest survival. Most realistic survival game — manage sanity, infections, shelter.
Survive on a raft in the ocean. Grow it into a floating city with friends. Relaxing and deep.
Neon wasteland shooter with the best gunplay in any open world. Fast, brutal, gorgeous colors.
Saints Row spin-off with hero-switching mechanics. Seoul open world, underrated chaos game.
Rip and tear resurrected. The FPS revival. Fast, brutal, no regenerating health — masterclass in design.
Combat as movement puzzle. The most mechanically demanding and rewarding FPS ever made.
The shooter that proved single-player campaigns still matter. Emotional, brutal, brilliant story.
BJ Blazkowicz in Nazi America. Incredible story, great gunplay, stylish and unforgettable.
Post-nuclear Moscow metro. Survival horror FPS with atmosphere unlike anything else on PC.
The atmospheric peak of the Metro series. Story, tension, and world-building at their finest.
Metro goes open world. Train journey across post-nuclear Russia — stunning varied environments.
Spread managed democracy. Co-op third-person shooter with stratagem system. 2024 phenomenon.
SWAT tactical shooter. Plan, breach, neutralize. The most tense co-op experience on PC.
PvPvE bounty hunting in 1895 Louisiana. Tension through the roof — every session is a story.
Scavenge moons for the Company. Horror co-op with procedural monsters. Friendship destroyer.
Destruction-based team shooter. Every wall is destroyable. Free to play, incredibly fresh gameplay.
Tactical mil-sim shooter. One-shot kills, minimal HUD — the most realistic feeling FPS on PC.
The peak Battlefield. 64-player conquest, levelution, spectacular set pieces. Still populated.
WWI Battlefield. Most emotional BF story. Operations mode at scale is completely unmatched.
The FPS that changed everything. Killstreak system, prestige, All Ghillied Up — iconic forever.
Modern reboot with great gunplay. DMZ extraction mode, Al-Mazrah open world map.
Free to play space ninja looter shooter. Thousands of hours of content if you fully dive in.
The remaster that added co-op campaign. Tight arena combat, atmospheric Gothic horror, perfect feel.
Best Gears since 2. Kait's story, open world sections, 4-player co-op. Criminally underrated.
Series revival. JD Fenix, new Swarm enemies, excellent Horde mode is the star of the show.
Rapture from the Big Daddy perspective. Eleanor's story, multiplayer, underrated in the series.
Faithful remake of the cyberpunk classic. SHODAN is terrifying. Grandfather of BioShock.
Rhythm action game where everything moves to the beat. Microsoft's surprise drop of the decade.
100-player military teamwork simulator. Communication and coordination win games here.
FromSoftware's magnum opus. Open world Souls with Miyazaki and George R.R. Martin. Unmissable.
The divisive Souls. Unique NG+ system, best DLC in the series, deeply underappreciated depth.
Gothic Lovecraftian horror action RPG. The fastest FromSoftware game. An absolute masterpiece.
Colonial-era fantasy RPG. Small studio, big ambition — faction politics and great companion system.
Deep CRPG with mythic powers. Crusade management, branching story, hundreds of build options.
Pirate CRPG with ship combat. Gorgeous writing, deep lore, excellent companion stories.
CRPG where evil has already won. Play as a servant of the overlord — brilliant moral complexity.
Spiritual Planescape successor. Philosophical sci-fantasy with combat as last resort.
Post-apocalyptic open world with zero hand-holding. Build a squad, found a city, survive everything.
The CRPG revival. Environmental interactions, co-op puzzles, great humor — where DOS2 started.
Samurai souls-like in feudal Japan. William Adams' story, deep ki-pulse system, 100+ hours.
Team Ninja's masterpiece. Deeper than Dark Souls with ki pulse. Co-op for all missions.
Pinocchio Souls-like. Best non-FromSoftware game in the genre. Incredible weapon crafting system.
Souls-like third-person shooter. Procedural world, co-op up to 3 players, great build variety.
Sequel that surpasses the original. Archetype system, stunning worlds, 3-player co-op throughout.
Dual-world Souls-like. Switch between living and dead realms. Beautiful and punishing throughout.
Anime Dark Souls with blood code system. Deep customization, co-op, great post-apocalyptic atmosphere.
Political vampire RPG. Three characters, three interweaving stories in Boston. Very choice-heavy.
Cyberpunk meets fantasy turn-based RPG. Hacker elves, samurai trolls — brilliant unique setting.
Best Shadowrun game. Hong Kong neon and spirit world. Incredible writing from start to finish.
Cyberpunk detective noir. Jack into criminal minds, explore dystopian Krakow. Rutger Hauer.
The RTS revival done right. Eight asymmetric civilizations, ranked ladder, documentary cinematics.

GAMES.t: Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition
GAMES.d: The greatest RTS of all time, remastered. 45 civilizations, ranked ladder, thousands of hours.
WWII RTS with destructible environments and tactical pause. Italy and North Africa campaigns.
WWII grand strategy. Control any nation, reshape history. Mod scene is absolutely incredible.
4X space grand strategy. Build an empire, encounter alien civilizations, ascend to godhood.
Paradox grand strategy in the 1800s. Industrialization, politics, pops — the deepest sim.
Amplitude's Civilization alternative. Mix cultures across eras, shape your unique nation.
3D space RTS with a devastating story. One of gaming's most emotionally powerful experiences.
Build and automate a factory on an alien planet. The most addictive game ever made. Period.
First-person factory builder on an alien planet. Co-op with friends becomes a complete obsession.
Build a galactic industrial empire around a star. Visually stunning factory game with exploration.
Colony survival storytelling simulator. Every playthrough generates a completely unique narrative.
The original colony sim. Infinite depth, procedural history, legendary failures. Losing is fun.
Space colony survival with real thermodynamics. Manage duplicants, air, water, heat — brilliant.
Steampunk RTS survival. One breach and your entire colony collapses. Brutal and satisfying.
Society-scale decisions in a frozen world. Bigger, more complex, even more morally brutal.
Roguelite city builder. Each settlement is a run — build, adapt, survive the eternal storm.

GAMES.t: Mutant Year Zero: Road to Eden
GAMES.d: XCOM meets stealth in post-apocalypse. Anthropomorphic mutants, great atmosphere, unique hybrid.
Submarine crew co-op survival under Europa's ocean. Roles matter, pressure kills, trust is tested.
1920+ diesel-punk RTS with mechs. Alternate history Europe, great campaign and multiplayer.
Overcooked meets base building. Design your restaurant then cook in it. Couch co-op perfection.
Every board game virtual. Catan, poker, chess, D&D — thousands of community-made games.
GT3 racing simulation. The most accurate racing game ever made. VR support is incredible.
Most authentic rally game. No assists, no mercy — master it and nothing compares. Pure skill.
Arcade time trial racing with insane community tracks. Free to play entry. Competitive and fun.
Soft body physics crash simulator. Every crash is physically simulated. Unreal and deeply addictive.
Best drift game on PC. Realistic tire model, great tracks, active competitive drift community.
Night vs day risk-reward system. Best NFS since Most Wanted. Night cops are absolutely brutal.
Anime-inspired NFS with cel-shaded effects. ASAP Rocky soundtrack. Best modern Need for Speed.
Hawaii open world racing festival. Huge variety of vehicle classes, playlist events, great fun.
Extreme sports open world — bike, ski, wingsuit, snowboard. Massive multiplayer mass races.
The sim racing reboot. Car mastery system, dynamic rubber buildup, 500+ cars on real tracks.
Official F1 simulation with all teams and tracks. Career mode, My Team, multiplayer ranked.
Soviet sci-fi BioShock-like. Stunning aesthetics, robot enemies, unique retro-futurist setting.
Groundhog Day assassination puzzle. Combine powers, break the loop, Arkane at their most stylish.
Yakuza spin-off as a detective. Two fighting styles, deep mystery story in Kamurocho.
Sequel with improved combat and skateboarding. School investigation mechanics are genuinely unique.
Yakuza in Edo-period Japan. Samurai Ryoma story with action combat instead of turn-based.

GAMES.t: Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain
GAMES.d: Open world stealth masterpiece. Afghanistan, D-Dog, Quiet — incredible systems depth.
Last great Splinter Cell. Spy vs Merc multiplayer returns, excellent co-op missions included.
Standalone expansion. Billie Lurk's story, bank heist mission — best single Dishonored level.
Cyberpunk parkour sequel. Motorbike levels, new abilities, same one-hit-kill flow state.
Souls-influenced Star Wars action. Cal Kestis, Force powers, incredible planet set pieces.
Sequel that expands everything. More Force powers, new planet Koboh, emotional continuation.
Amicia and Hugo flee the Inquisition through rat-infested France. Emotional, cinematic, beautiful.
Stunning sequel. More rats, more emotion, incredible graphics. One of the best looking games ever.
Shadow stealth ninja game with 3-player co-op. Kill from darkness, chain assassinations.
Worlds within worlds puzzle game. Created by Limbo/Inside dev. Jaw-dropping design throughout.
Manic SNES-style platformer. Peppino Spaghetti rescues his pizza. Speed, chaos, excellence.
